subject: Geology
title: Nuclear Waste Storage
description: A quick, practical guide to Nuclear Waste Storage.

hook.text: Nuclear waste stays dangerous for thousands of years. The challenge is finding rock stable enough to contain it.

buildup.text: Deep geologic repositories bury waste in stable rock formations far from groundwater and seismic activity.

discovery.text: Finland's Onkalo facility is the world's first permanent nuclear waste repository, built into 1.8 billion-year-old rock.

twist.text: Choosing a site is as much political as geologic. Communities resist hosting waste even when the geology is ideal.

climax.text: Nuclear waste storage demands the longest-term thinking in engineering. Geology provides the only proven container.

punchline.text: Old rock guards the most dangerous waste.

quiz.question: Why was Finland's Onkalo site chosen for nuclear waste?
options: It has active volcanic heat to destroy waste
Its 1.8 billion-year-old rock is extremely stable
It is located under the ocean
